content: Just as the 'reflection' defined in high school English vocabulary acts as a bidirectional mapping that mirrors the external world onto internal thought and allows writers to refine their ideas through iterative review, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by formalizing the linguistic dual nature of reflection: treating reflection not just as an error-correction step, but as a lexical 'mirror mapping' that converts the LLM's implicit unstructured outputs into explicit, reviewable internal tokens aligned with external performance standards.
